title: Electrical and Electronic Engineering Technologists and Technicians
description: Apply electrical and electronic theory and related knowledge, usually under the direction of engineering staff, to design, build, repair, adjust, and modify electrical components, circuitry, controls, and machinery for subsequent evaluation and use by engineering staff in making engineering design decisions.

tasks.text: Modify, maintain, or repair electronics equipment or systems to ensure proper functioning.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Replace defective components or parts, using hand tools and precision instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up and operate specialized or standard test equipment to diagnose, test, or analyze the performance of electronic components, assemblies, or systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Read blueprints, wiring diagrams, schematic drawings, or engineering instructions for assembling electronics units, applying knowledge of electronic theory and components.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Identify and resolve equipment malfunctions, working with manufacturers or field representatives as necessary to procure replacement parts.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assemble electrical systems or prototypes, using hand tools or measuring instruments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review electrical engineering plans to ensure adherence to design specifications and compliance with applicable electrical codes and standards.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Assemble, test, or maintain circuitry or electronic components, according to engineering instructions, technical manuals, or knowledge of electronics, using hand or power tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review existing electrical engineering criteria to identify necessary revisions, deletions, or amendments to outdated material.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Maintain system logs or manuals to document testing or operation of equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Select electronics equipment, components, or systems to meet functional specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Calculate design specifications or cost, material, and resource estimates, and prepare project schedules and budgets.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Educate equipment operators on the proper use of equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Supervise the installation or operation of electronic equipment or systems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Compile and maintain records documenting engineering schematics, installed equipment, installation or operational problems, resources used, repairs, or corrective action performed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Modify electrical prototypes, parts, assemblies, or systems to correct functional deviations.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Integrate software or hardware components, using computer, microprocessor, or control architecture.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Procure parts and maintain inventory and related documentation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Participate in training or continuing education activities to stay abreast of engineering or industry advances.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Research equipment or component needs, sources, competitive prices, delivery times, or ongoing operational costs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide user applications or engineering support or recommendations for new or existing equipment with regard to installation, upgrades, or enhancements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Specify, coordinate, or conduct quality control or quality assurance programs or procedures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Produce electronics drawings or other graphics representing industrial control, instrumentation, sensors, or analog or digital telecommunications networks, using computer-aided design (CAD) software.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Install or maintain electrical control systems, industrial automation systems, or electrical equipment, including control circuits, variable speed drives, or programmable logic controllers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Design or modify engineering schematics for electrical transmission and distribution systems or for electrical installation in residential, commercial, or industrial buildings, using computer-aided design (CAD) software.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Interpret test information to resolve design-related problems.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Conduct statistical studies to analyze or compare production costs for sustainable or nonsustainable designs.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Construct and evaluate electrical components for consumer electronics applications such as fuel cells for consumer electronic devices, power saving devices for computers or televisions, or energy efficient power chargers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Participate in the development or testing of electrical aspects of new green technologies, such as lighting, optical data storage devices, and energy efficient televisions.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Review, develop, or prepare maintenance standards.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.
